item.title: La Favorita
item.description: It’s been a wonderful morning. I took a nice walk outside, taking in the fresh breeze and enjoying the early spring weather, windy as it was. Then came this wonderful piece of music on the radio, La Favorite, the grand opera in four acts by Gaetano Donizetti, frequently also known by its Italian...

item.title: Daylight Saving Time
item.description: British Columbia’s final time change has occurred today, March 8, 2026. Premier David Eby announced on March 2nd that BC will permanently adopt daylight saving time, ending the twice-a-year clock changes. On November 1, 2026, clocks will remain the same, completing the transition to a year-round...

item.title: Negative Experiences
item.description: Love, friendship, and respect do not unite people as much as a shared hatred (Chekhov, n.d.). This observation is unsettling because a negative force like hatred can be more cohesive than a positive one. It suggests that what we oppose can define us more powerfully than what we support.

item.title: Self-control
item.description: Train your mind, and you can shape the world around you. The mind’s true power lies not in suppressing spontaneous, primal impulses, but in transforming them from driving forces into forces you can steer. Emotion becomes intelligence, reaction becomes strategy, and spontaneity becomes a tool....

item.title: Writing For Life
item.description: Textbooks are usually written in a linear and sequential order designed for training and learning. Real life is entirely different; it requires you to apply your training to non-linear and unpredictable events and circumstances. This raises the question of whether the conventional approach to...

item.title: Dependencies
item.description: Sometimes we create unnecessary dependencies that quietly shape our workflow, causing delays, friction, and anxiety. Like premature optimization, adding structure before it’s needed can actively work against the work itself. The more conditions that must align before progress is possible, the...

item.title: Pareto Principle
item.description: The Pareto Principle, also known as the “80:20 rule,” asserts that 80% of outcomes stem from 20% of causes. Named after the Italian polymath Vilfredo Pareto, the principle is supported by empirical observation showing that this 80:20 distribution applies to a wide variety of situations,...

item.title: On Stupidty
item.description: The basic laws assert that, according to Cipolla (Cipolla, 2019) : 1. Everyone inevitably underestimates the number of stupid individuals in circulation. 2. The probability of an individual being stupid is independent of any characteristic of that person. 3. A stupid person is one who causes...

item.title: On Bullshit
item.description: Bullshit is a technical term, and Harry Frankfurt’s book (Frankfurt, 2005) is probably the best reference for it. When someone lies, they knowingly misrepresent the truth. However, when someone bullshits, they don’t care about the truth; they simply say whatever is necessary to achieve their...

item.title: Now
item.description: I am currently focused solely on transitioning from my current job to my new one. While it is not a huge task, it is taking a lot of mental energy. If I had had less time before the transfer, it would have been much easier, being a completionist making it more difficult than it should be.

item.title: Scenes From Above
item.description: Julian Lage quietly released a new album, Scenes From Above, on January 23, 2026. The music is wonderful, and it’s especially special because it features another talented musician, the keyboardist John Medeski. We also get to enjoy his long-term bandmates, the bassist Jorge Roeder and the...

item.title: My Way *and* the Highway
item.description: Man makes tools. In turn, tools make a different man through his work. Neither the man nor the tool remains the same; they are forever transformed, locked in a reciprocal process of mutual becoming, thereby perpetually evolving.

item.title: Ever So Slowly
item.description: During one summer, I had the opportunity to review my guitar practices that I recorded since 2018 and a few earlier than that. I named the playlist Ever So Slowly, because it’s been such a slow process for me to make progress on the guitar. That said, the playlist also demonstrates the fact that...

item.title: Rick Rubin
item.description: Rick Rubin is a remarkable man who has produced such notable artists as Johnny Cash and Jake Bugg. I recently came across The Way of Code (Anthropic, n.d.), in which he notes, “Each encounter I’ve had with Lao Tzu has pointed to something new. Almost as if the book changes with every reading. I...

Another year is about to conclude. It passed like we inhaled a breath of fresh air yesterday, and today we are exhaling it, a little tired but filled with hope and excitement. Looking back, the year has been, despite its tiring parts, also full of joyful periods, growth, and satisfaction.

item.title: Emptiness
item.description: Clay transforms into pottery through craft, but it’s the emptiness within that makes a pot truly useful. What’s absent can be just as significant as what’s present. This idea originates from the Tao Te Ching, a foundational text of Taoism. (Tzu & Needleman, 1989)

item.title: White Christmas
item.description: I started learning this song in December 2022, approaching it one phrase at a time and at a very gradual pace in the weeks leading up to Christmas. In retrospect, I might have chosen a particularly challenging arrangement, but after dedicating so much time and effort to it, I feel compelled to...

item.title: Notes
item.description: No description available.

item.title: Telecaster
item.description: The rosewood fingerboard and the U-shaped maple neck immediately caught my eye. I recognized this fine instrument instantly; it was the same Telecaster we had seen during our visit to Tokyo last year. And now, here I was, holding it in my hands, this time at the Fender Flagship Store in...

item.title: Quiet Mornings
item.description: This morning, I experienced deep satisfaction in rediscovering the tastes, sounds, and sensations I can effortlessly perceive, which profoundly affect my mind. Many advocate practicing gratitude to achieve such effects, yet prescriptive approaches may diminish the impact of these effects....
